question.label: Growth blockers
question.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
question.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

answer: I am still motivated, but the promotion outcome made me more careful about where I spend political capital.
note: Promotion disappointment has made him more cautious about spending political capital, signalling a strong growth blocker and mild uncertainty about how to advance.

question.label: Political capital
question.name: Political capital — where are you holding back right now?
question.description: Drills into the specific arenas where the promotion outcome made him less willing to take visible positions.

answer: I can lead major initiatives, but I do not always translate that into visible strategic influence.
note: He names a clear growth gap: strong initiative leadership is not yet turning into visible strategic influence.

question.label: Strategic influence gap
question.name: Where specifically does your influence stop short of the strategic level?
question.description: Drills into the exact boundary between leading initiatives and shaping broader decisions.

answer: I get positive feedback on craft and collaboration, but less clarity on what separates me at the next level.
note: He names a clear growth gap: positive feedback on craft and collaboration, but unclear differentiation for the next level; also reveals a clarity issue worth probing later.

question.label: Stretch now
question.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?
question.description: Distinguishes comfort-zone execution from genuine growth moments.

answer: Sometimes I avoid high-ambiguity forums because I do not want to sound underprepared.
note: Avoiding high-ambiguity forums to avoid sounding underprepared signals a strong growth constraint and mild pullback from visible engagement.

question.label: High-ambiguity forums
question.name: High-ambiguity forums — what feels most risky when you step into them?
question.description: Drills into the specific fear or constraint behind avoiding visible strategic settings.

answer: I think I need to own a cross-org decision area, not just deliver strong design outcomes.
note: He names a concrete next-level aspiration — owning a cross-org decision area — which signals strong growth direction and some continued ambition.

question.label: Cross-org decisions
question.name: Cross-org decision area — which decisions do you want your name attached to?
question.description: Turns his stated aspiration into a concrete picture of the scope and outcomes he wants to own.

answer: I also need clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set.
note: Names missing sponsorship in priority-setting rooms as a concrete growth blocker; also hints at clarity/alignment, but clarity is off-signature. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set' not drilled because drill cap requires stage advance.

question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

answer: If we define two concrete leadership bets for this half, I can commit fully.
note: Names a missing condition for committing fully — two concrete leadership bets — which signals stalled growth direction and some hesitancy; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'two concrete leadership bets for this half'.

question.label: Sponsorship shape
question.name: What kind of sponsorship would actually move the needle for you here?
question.description: Surfaces the specific air cover, introductions, or backing he needs instead of generic support.

answer: I want milestones that show progress before the next cycle, not only retrospective feedback.
note: Names a specific missing structure — forward milestones instead of retrospective feedback — which signals low clarity and mildly reduced engagement; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] milestones before the next cycle.

question.label: First move
question.name: What's the first concrete thing you want to do before we next meet?
question.description: Closes on a specific commitment that turns the growth conversation into an action plan.

note: [SKIP] no signal — planner bypassed, queue carried forward
